questions.original_question: 介绍一下吉利控股？吉利控股给路特斯科技带来什么助力？
questions.answer:
吉利控股集团（Geely Holding）是一家全球领先的移动技术集团，以其成功孵化和振兴多个世界知名汽车品牌而著称，包括沃尔沃、极星、领克和极氪等。作为吉利控股旗下唯一拥有跑车基因的品牌，路特斯科技（Lotus Technology）在吉利控股的全面支持下，正从一家英国跑车公司转型为全球高性能电动汽车的先驱，致力于弥合传统跑车与新一代电动汽车之间的差距。

吉利控股为路特斯科技提供了全方位的助力，涵盖制造、供应链、研发、物流基础设施和人力资源等多个领域。具体而言，吉利控股负责原材料的订购和检验、生产计划、生产质量控制、制造车辆的物流和运输，以及制造工厂的建设和运营。此外，吉利控股还协助获取制造车辆所需的证书。通过技术许可、生产合作和供应链框架等协议，路特斯科技能够充分利用吉利控股的丰富资源，快速扩大商业化规模，同时保持轻资产运营模式。这种合作模式不仅减少了路特斯科技的前期资本投入，还帮助其有效控制供应链相关风险，加速产品开发，并在全球市场上实现更快速、高效和成本效益的扩展。

总之，吉利控股的全面支持为路特斯科技的转型和全球化发展提供了强有力的保障，使其能够在竞争激烈的高性能电动汽车市场中占据领先地位。

rag_info.chunk_content: Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr. Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development, logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between traditional sports cars and a new generation of electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the business combination with LCAA, a SPAC affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable the company to effectively raise brand awareness globally. L Catterton has an exclusive relationship for consumer-focused private equity with LVMH and Financière Agache, the main investment company of Bernard Arnault, LVMH’s chairman and chief executive officer, and his family.

rag_info.sub_query: What is Geely Holding?

rag_info.chunk_content: Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr. Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between traditional sports cars and a new generation of electric vehicles. Eletre is a luxury lifestyle E-segment SUV powered by Lotus Technology's 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture, or EPA. Combining its technologically advanced platform with cutting-edge design, Eletre delivers leading performance in acceleration, driving range, and charging speed. Lotus Technology offers three different versions of Eletre, namely, Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R, to satisfy the various demands of customers. Eletre R, in particular, generates a maximum of 905 horsepower (hp) and can accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h in 2.95 seconds.
rag_info.sub_query: What is Geely Holding?

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology believes that its close relationship with Geely Holding provides the company with a unique competitive advantage in its ability to rapidly scale commercialization while maintaining an asset-light operating model with less upfront capital expenditure commitment than other original equipment manufacturers (OEMs). Lotus Technology has entered into a variety of agreements, including agreements related to technology licensing, manufacturing cooperation, and supply of framework, among others, with Geely Holding. The strategic partnership with Geely Holding allows Lotus Technology to effectively control supply chain-related risks and accelerate product development. Lotus Technology's ability to successfully operate its Global Commercial Platform will affect its ability to increase revenues. Lotus Technology has taken steps to diversify its revenue sources, for example, by entering into the Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK, pursuant to which Lotus Technology is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the U.S., where Lotus Technology International Limited will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute Lotus UK vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Technology.
rag_info.sub_query: What is Geely Holding?

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology has established a strategic collaborative relationship with Geely Holding, and Lotus Technology's asset-light business model is supported by Geely Holding's ecosystem partners. Lotus Technology expects the partnership with Geely Holding to allow the company to bring its vehicles to the market at an accelerated pace by leveraging Geely Holding’s manufacturing capacity, bargaining power in procurement and supply chain, capital investment, and operational support. Lotus Technology entered into a manufacturing arrangement with Geely Holding for the manufacture of the Eletre and future models for 10 years starting from 2022. Pursuant to the manufacturing agreement, Lotus Technology commissioned Geely Holding for the production of the Eletre and future models and authorized Geely Holding to access Lotus Technology's technologies for the production of such models. Lotus Technology is mainly responsible for the design and development of the models, designation of suppliers, product announcement, and ensuring consistency with global standards of the Lotus brand. Lotus Technology also provides Geely Holding with the necessary intellectual properties for the manufacture of the Eletre and future models. Geely Holding is mainly responsible for the ordering and inspection of raw materials, production planning, production quality control, logistics and transportation of manufactured vehicles, and construction and operation of the manufacturing plant. Particularly, quality control is carried out in accordance with Lotus Technology's quality assurance framework and approved by Geely Holding. In addition, Geely Holding is responsible for obtaining certificates for the manufactured vehicles.

title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the Overview Chapter**

We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the iconic British brand "Lotus," which boasts over 70 years of racing heritage. Since our acquisition by Geely Holding in 2017, we have been transitioning from a traditional sports car company to a luxury lifestyle BEV maker, leveraging Geely's extensive support in manufacturing, supply chain, and research and development (R&D).

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is projected to experience significant growth, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, ultimately reaching nearly 1.9 million units by 2031. Currently, this market is underserved, featuring only about 10 luxury BEV models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models.

Our first fully electric model, the Eletre, launched in 2022, is a luxury E-segment SUV powered by our proprietary 800-volt Electric Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. We aim to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, having launched our second lifestyle vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024. Future product launches include a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026.

Our R&D capabilities serve as a competitive strength, supported by five facilities located in the U.K., Germany, and China. These facilities focus on developing advanced technologies, including a leading Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS). We manufacture our BEV models in partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China, with a planned annual production capacity of 150,000 units.

We employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, we successfully delivered 6,970 vehicles, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models. Our collaboration with L Catterton, which has connections to LVMH, is anticipated to enhance our brand awareness and consumer insights, although no formal agreements with LVMH have been finalized as of this prospectus.

This strategic positioning within the luxury BEV market, combined with our innovative product offerings and robust R&D capabilities, positions us well for future growth and success in the evolving automotive landscape.

title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Major Related Parties Associated with Geely Holding**

Geely Holding, a prominent player in the automotive industry, operates through a complex network of related parties that include subsidiaries, joint ventures, and affiliated entities. This extensive framework supports Geely's diverse operations in manufacturing, research and development, technology solutions, and automotive components.

1. **Parent Company**: 
   - **Geely Holding** serves as the parent company, overseeing the entire corporate structure and strategic direction.

2. **Key Subsidiaries and Joint Ventures**:
   - **Zhejiang Geely Automobile Co., Ltd.**: The central manufacturing entity responsible for producing a wide range of vehicles.
   - **Wuhan Geely Auto Parts Co., Ltd.**: Supplies essential automotive components, ensuring the production line is well-equipped.
   - **Ningbo Geely R&D**: Focuses on research and development, driving innovation within the company.
   - **Zhejiang Liankong Technologies Co., Ltd.**: Engaged in providing advanced technology solutions, enhancing Geely's technological capabilities.
   - **Ecarx**: Specializes in automotive technology, contributing to the development of smart vehicle systems.

3. **Investment and Distribution Entities**:
   - **LGIL**: Acts as a significant investment arm, managing Geely's financial interests and investments.
   - **Polestar Automotive China Distribution Co., Ltd.**: Concentrates on the distribution of electric performance vehicles, aligning with the global shift towards sustainable transportation.
   - **Lotus Cars USA Inc.**: Represents the Lotus brand in the U.S. market, expanding Geely's footprint in the luxury sports car segment.

4. **International Partnerships**:
   - **Volvo Car Corporation**: A major international automotive brand under Geely's ownership, contributing to Geely's global presence and brand diversification.
   - **China Euro Vehicle Technology AB**: Plays a role in the development of new vehicle platforms, enhancing Geely's technological edge in the automotive sector.

5. **Additional Notable Entities**:
   - Other significant related parties include **Zhejiang Jirun Automobile Co., Ltd.** and **Hangzhou Xuanyu Human Resources Co., Ltd.**, among others, which further illustrate the breadth of Geely's operations.

The relationships within this network are influenced significantly by the controlling shareholder of Geely, who maintains a substantial impact over these related parties. This interconnected structure emphasizes Geely's extensive involvement in both domestic and international automotive markets, showcasing a wide range of operations from manufacturing and sales to cutting-edge research and development.

In summary, Geely Holding's major related parties reflect a robust and diverse ecosystem that underpins its strategic initiatives and operational capabilities in the global automotive industry.

Lotus Technology aims to leverage its partners’ industry expertise to ensure that each vehicle produced meets the company's strict quality standards. Lotus Technology's Collaboration with Geely Holding Lotus Technology has established a strategic collaborative relationship with its major shareholder, Geely Holding, and its asset-light business model is supported by the Geely Holding ecosystem partners. Lotus Technology expects its relationship with Geely Holding to allow the company to bring its vehicles to the market at an accelerated pace by leveraging Geely Holding’s manufacturing capacity, bargaining power in procurement and supply chain, capital investment, and operational support. Lotus Technology entered into a manufacturing arrangement with Geely Holding for the manufacture of its vehicles for 10 years starting from 2022. Pursuant to the manufacturing agreement, Lotus Technology commissioned Geely Holding for vehicle production and authorized Geely Holding to access its technologies for the production of such models. Lotus Technology is mainly responsible for the design and development of the models, designation of suppliers, product announcement, and ensuring consistency with global standards of the Lotus brand. Lotus Technology also provides Geely Holding with the intellectual properties necessary for vehicle production. Geely Holding is mainly responsible for the ordering and inspection of raw materials, production planning, production quality control, logistics and transportation of manufactured vehicles, and construction and operation of the manufacturing plant.

summary: Our collaboration with Geely Holding is a strategic partnership that plays a crucial role in our vehicle development process. By leveraging Geely's manufacturing capabilities and market presence alongside our technological advancements, we aim to drive innovation and accelerate the transition to sustainable mobility solutions, including electric vehicles. summary: The organization has formed various agreements with Geely Holding, covering areas such as research and development, procurement, manufacturing, engineering, IT, and human resources. This partnership is crucial for the company's operations, enabling it to leverage Geely's extensive automotive industry experience while focusing on its strengths in design, technology integration, and sustainability. However, the reliance on Geely also introduces significant operational risks, including the potential inability to operate independently, launch new vehicle models, and meet production goals. The company faces challenges such as the risk of delays, cost overruns, and quality assurance issues, which could adversely affect its financial condition and growth prospects. Disputes with Geely or negative perceptions of its vehicle quality could further impact the company's brand. Moreover, the inability to maintain this collaboration may hinder the establishment of new agreements with third-party manufacturers, complicating operations and financial performance. The partnership also influences supply chain efficiency, as Geely's bargaining power aids in securing favorable terms with suppliers. A breakdown in this relationship could lead to difficulties in supplier negotiations and increased costs due to price fluctuations. Overall, the organization’s reliance on Geely Holding is a double-edged sword, providing essential support while posing significant risks to its operational and financial stability.

There is a risk of potential disputes with Geely Holding, and Lotus Technology could be affected by adverse publicity related to Geely Holding whether or not such publicity is related to their collaboration with Lotus Technology.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control 
summary: The "Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control" chapter highlights the critical role of manufacturers and suppliers in the vehicle development process, emphasizing the necessity of strict adherence to quality standards. A strategic partnership with Geely Holding, the major shareholder, underpins an asset-light business model that accelerates market entry by leveraging Geely's extensive manufacturing capacity, procurement power, capital investment, and operational support.

In 2022, a significant milestone was reached with the establishment of a 10-year manufacturing agreement with Geely Holding for vehicle production. This agreement delineates responsibilities, with the company retaining control over design, development, supplier designation, product announcements, and the maintenance of Lotus brand standards. Conversely, Geely Holding is responsible for sourcing raw materials, production planning, quality control, logistics, and plant operations, ensuring that quality assurance aligns with the company's established framework.

The manufacturing facility located in Wuhan, China, owned by Geely Holding, is specifically tailored for electric vehicles (EVs) and spans over one million square meters. This state-of-the-art plant is equipped with advanced manufacturing technologies, including stamping, welding, painting, assembly, and a dedicated quality inspection center. Additionally, the facility integrates intelligent driving technology for vehicle transport within workshops and features a 3 km track for quality testing, enabling high-speed evaluations of up to 230 km/h and providing various driving experiences for customers.

Overall, this chapter underscores the strategic collaboration with Geely Holding as a cornerstone of the manufacturing and supply chain strategy, ensuring that the company can efficiently produce high-quality vehicles while maintaining brand integrity and meeting market demands.

title: The automotive market is highly competitive, and we may not be successful in competing in this industry. 
summary: The global automotive market is highly competitive, characterized by significant barriers to entry such as high capital requirements, long vehicle development lead times, specialized expertise, regulatory compliance, and the establishment of brand identity and sales networks. The company has strategically positioned itself in the sustainable luxury Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) sector, facing unique challenges including the high costs of developing competitive electric powertrains, inexperience in servicing electric vehicles, regulatory issues concerning battery transport, and the necessity for adequate charging infrastructure. The competitive landscape is expected to intensify with new entrants, not only from traditional Internal Combustion Engine (ICE) vehicles but also from other new energy vehicles.

The company faces formidable competition, particularly from international players with greater financial, technological, and manufacturing resources, which may impede its ability to compete effectively. Key competitive factors include local protectionism, brand recognition, product quality, technological innovation, pricing, and customer service. Increased competition could result in reduced vehicle sales and higher inventory levels, adversely affecting the company's financial health and market share.

The company's reliance on partnerships, particularly with Geely Holding for research, development, manufacturing, and engineering, introduces operational risks and challenges related to independence and production targets. Critical agreements, such as the Technology License Agreement and Manufacturing Cooperation Agreement with Geely, are vital for leveraging Geely's expertise while focusing on differentiation in design and technology. However, this reliance also exposes the company to potential disputes and negative publicity that could impact its reputation and operational capabilities.

Founded in 2018, the company launched its first vehicle, the Eletre, in 2022, resulting in a limited operating history that complicates revenue predictions and the timely delivery of high-quality vehicles. The company's sustainability is contingent upon effectively executing its development and manufacturing plans, particularly at its Wuhan facility, which is essential for producing new BEV models. Challenges include potential delays in technology development, funding shortages, supply chain disruptions, quality control issues, and compliance with regulations.

Financially, the company has not achieved profitability, reporting net losses of $750.3 million in 2023, $724.6 million in 2022, and $110.5 million in 2021. Negative cash flows from operations were $386.9 million in 2023, $351.4 million in 2022, and $126.5 million in 2021. The accumulated deficit as of December 31 for each year stood at $1.6 billion in 2023, $846.8 million in 2022, and $122.8 million in 2021. Capital expenditures were $213.7 million in 2023, $133.3 million in 2022, and $34.6 million in 2021. The company has primarily financed its operations through the issuance of exchangeable and convertible notes and related party borrowings. 

To sustain operations over the next 12 months, the company requires additional liquidity and is exploring various funding strategies, including bank loans, related party loans, and operational improvements. However, the feasibility of these strategies is uncertain and may be affected by external factors. Future financing may be necessary to meet capital requirements or address unforeseen challenges, but there is no guarantee that financing will be available on acceptable terms. The company faces pressure to generate positive cash flow due to contractual obligations, including capital commitments and debts. Investments in production, sales, and R&D may not yield timely revenue increases or positive cash flow, raising substantial doubts about the company's ability to continue as a going concern. The financial statements do not reflect potential adjustments if the company cannot operate normally, which could lead to significant investor losses. Overall, the company must navigate these competitive and operational challenges while maintaining strong partnerships and brand integrity to secure its future in the automotive market.

title: Recently Issued Accounting Pronouncements 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Recently Issued Accounting Pronouncements and Leadership Team**

This summary provides an overview of the recently issued accounting pronouncements relevant to the company, as detailed in Note 2 of the consolidated and combined financial statements included in the prospectus. The company is classified as an emerging growth entity under the JOBS Act, reporting revenues of less than $1.235 billion for the fiscal year 2023. This classification allows the company certain exemptions that facilitate its financial reporting and compliance obligations.

**Leadership Team Overview:**

1. **Daniel Donghui Li**, 53, has served as the Chairman of the Board since November 2021. He brings extensive experience from Geely Holding Group, where he has held significant roles including Vice President, CFO, and CEO. He holds an MBA from Indiana University, along with degrees in management engineering and philosophy.

2. **Qingfeng Feng**, 51, is the Director and CEO, having been with the company since its inception. He has been part of Geely Holding since 1999, occupying various leadership positions such as Vice President and Chief Technical Officer. He holds a degree in Chemical Engineering and an Executive MBA from Tsinghua University.

3. **Alexious Kuen Long Lee**, 48, has been the CFO since the company's inception and became a director in November 2021. He has a robust background in capital access and strategy, having previously held senior roles at FIAT Industrial and CLSA.

4. **Ooi Teik Huat**, 64, joined as a director in January 2023. He has a solid background in corporate finance and has served on the boards of several public companies. He earned a Bachelor of Economics from Monash University.

5. **Ning Yu**, 53, has been a director since July 2022 and possesses 30 years of experience in the automotive industry. He is the managing partner of Zhongqing Capital and has held senior positions at Geely and Fiat. He holds a Ph.D. in Automotive Engineering.

6. **Ada Yunfeng Yan**, 54, became an Independent Director in February 2024. She has a finance background with experience at ExxonMobil and Shell and currently runs a financial consulting business. She holds a degree in international finance and an MBA.

7. **Anish Melwani**, 45, also an Independent Director since February 2024, is the Chairman and CEO of LVMH for North America. He has a strong consulting background from McKinsey & Company and holds a B.A. in Economics from Harvard University.

This summary integrates critical information regarding the company’s recent accounting pronouncements and outlines the leadership team, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements while maintaining clarity and factual accuracy. The leadership structure reflects a diverse range of expertise, supporting the company's strategic direction and operational effectiveness.

title: Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control 
summary: Our collaboration with Geely Holding is a strategic partnership that plays a crucial role in our vehicle development process. By leveraging Geely's manufacturing capabilities and market presence alongside our technological advancements, we aim to drive innovation and accelerate the transition to sustainable mobility solutions, including electric vehicles. This partnership enhances our asset-light business model and facilitates the market introduction of our vehicles through Geely's procurement advantages and operational support. 

Under a 10-year manufacturing agreement initiated in 2022, Geely Holding has been authorized to access our technologies while we retain oversight of design, development, and brand standards. Geely is responsible for sourcing materials, production planning, quality control, and logistics, all while adhering to our quality assurance framework. This collaboration emphasizes research and development initiatives to create cutting-edge products that meet consumer demands and promote environmental sustainability, ultimately strengthening our competitive edge in the global automotive market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology seeks to partner with reputable suppliers. Lotus Technology leverages the Geely Holding ecosystem for the order of basic auto parts. Lotus Technology intends to use most of the same core suppliers for its vehicles. Lotus Technology has developed close relationships with key suppliers. These include NVIDIA, a global leader in AI computing, which provides its chips for the advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) used in Lotus Technology's vehicles; Qualcomm, a world-leading developer of semiconductor technologies, which provides 8155 smart cockpit chips; and CATL, a world-leading manufacturer of lithium-ion batteries, which provides batteries for Lotus Technology's battery electric vehicles (BEVs). Most of these suppliers are key partners in the Geely Holding ecosystem with years of strong partnership with Geely Holding. Lotus Technology follows its internal process to select suppliers, taking into account quality, cost, and timing. Lotus Technology has a part quality management team that is responsible for managing and ensuring that suppliers meet quality standards. Lotus Technology's method for selecting suppliers depends on the nature of the supplies needed. For general parts that are widely available, Lotus Technology examines proposals from multiple suppliers and chooses based on quality and price competitiveness, among other factors. For parts requiring special designs, Lotus Technology reviews design proposals and chooses largely based on design-related factors. However, in certain cases, Lotus Technology has limited choices given its scale, such as battery cell packages, so in such circumstances, Lotus Technology typically partners with suppliers that it believes to be well-positioned to meet its needs.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Suppliers 
summary: In the "Our Suppliers" chapter, the company outlines its strategic approach to supplier partnerships, emphasizing a commitment to working with reputable suppliers within the Geely Holding ecosystem, particularly for essential auto parts. Key suppliers include NVIDIA, which provides advanced driver-assistance system (ADAS) chips; Qualcomm, supplying the 8155 smart cockpit chips; and CATL, responsible for manufacturing lithium-ion batteries for battery electric vehicles (BEVs). These suppliers are integral to the company's operations and have established long-term partnerships that enhance the overall supply chain.

The supplier selection process is meticulously guided by internal criteria that prioritize quality, cost, and timing. A dedicated part quality management team is in place to ensure that all suppliers meet stringent quality standards. For general parts that are widely available, the company evaluates multiple proposals, focusing on both quality and price competitiveness. In contrast, the selection process for specialized parts is primarily driven by design proposals. In cases where options are limited, such as with battery cell packages, the company collaborates closely with suppliers that are well-positioned to meet its specific requirements.

Furthermore, the company integrates environmental guidelines into its supplier selection process, aligning with its Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) strategies and goals. This commitment to sustainability ensures that suppliers not only meet operational standards but also adhere to environmental best practices, reinforcing the company's dedication to responsible sourcing and corporate responsibility. Overall, the chapter highlights a comprehensive and structured approach to supplier management that balances quality, cost-effectiveness, and sustainability.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has entered into a variety of agreements, such as agreements related to research and development, procurement, manufacturing, engineering, and IT and human resource-related supporting services with its strategic partner, Geely Holding. Lotus Technology's reliance on these agreements subjects the company to a number of significant risks, including the risk of being unable to operate as a standalone business, launch new vehicles, reach development and production targets, or focus efforts on core areas of differentiation. Of particular importance for Lotus Technology's operations are the related party agreements with Geely Holding and its affiliate entities. These related party agreements include the Technology License Agreement, the Manufacture Cooperation Agreement, and the Supply of Framework Agreement, amongst other areas. These agreements are described in more detail in this annual report under “Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions—Related Party Transactions.” These partnerships permit Lotus Technology to benefit from Geely Holding’s decades of experience investing in established auto-manufacturers while focusing its efforts on core areas of differentiation, such as design, research and development, performance, and rapid adoption of the latest technologies and sustainability solutions. Lotus Technology intends to continue to rely on its partnership with Geely Holding as part of its strategy. Collaboration with Geely Holding for research and development, procurement, manufacturing, and engineering is subject to risks with respect to operations that are outside of Lotus Technology's control. Lotus Technology currently relies and expects to continue to rely on Geely Holding, in terms of research and development, procurement, manufacturing, and engineering with regard to its vehicles.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our reliance on a variety of arrangements with Geely Holding, such as agreements related to research and development, procurement, manufacturing, engineering, and IT and human resource related supporting service, could subject us to risks. 
summary: The company has formed several strategic agreements with Geely Holding, encompassing research and development, procurement, manufacturing, engineering, and IT and human resources. While these partnerships leverage Geely's extensive automotive expertise and allow the company to focus on its core strengths, they also introduce significant operational risks. The reliance on Geely for efficient production capabilities and component supplies raises concerns about the company's ability to operate independently, launch new vehicles, and meet development and production targets. Potential delays, cost overruns, and disputes with Geely could adversely affect the company's financial performance and brand perception, particularly regarding the quality of its luxury lifestyle vehicles.

The agreements, including the Technology License Agreement, Manufacture Cooperation Agreement, and Supply of Framework Agreement, are crucial for the company's operations, as they generate revenue from design and development services provided to Geely. However, the inability to maintain this partnership could hinder the establishment of new agreements with third-party manufacturers and impact operational independence. Additionally, the company's supply chain efficiency is heavily influenced by its relationship with Geely, which holds significant bargaining power. Any disruption in this partnership could jeopardize supplier relationships, leading to challenges in retaining current suppliers or securing favorable terms with new ones, and exposing the company to price fluctuations and supply disruptions. Overall, these factors present considerable risks to the company's business operations, financial condition, and future prospects.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology cannot provide any assurance as to whether Geely Holding will be able to develop efficient, automated, low-cost production capabilities and processes, and reliable sources of component supply that will enable Lotus Technology to meet the quality, price, engineering, design, and production standards, as well as the production volumes, required to successfully commercialize its vehicles. Even if Geely Holding is successful in developing high volume production capabilities and processes and reliably sourcing its component supplies, no assurance can be given as to whether Geely Holding will be able to do so in a manner that avoids significant delays and cost overruns, including as a result of factors beyond its and Lotus Technology's control, such as problems with suppliers and vendors, or force majeure events, or in time to meet Lotus Technology's commercialization schedules or to satisfy the requirements of existing and potential customers. Any failure to develop such production processes and capabilities within projected costs and timelines could have a material and adverse effect on Lotus Technology's business, results of operations, financial condition, and prospects. There is a risk of potential disputes with Geely Holding, and Lotus Technology could be affected by adverse publicity related to Geely Holding whether or not such publicity is related to their collaboration. Lotus Technology's ability to successfully build a luxury lifestyle vehicle brand could also be adversely affected by perceptions about the quality of Geely Holding’s vehicles.

Lotus Technology has entered into a variety of agreements, including agreements related to technology licensing, manufacturing cooperation, and supply of framework, among others, with Geely Holding. Lotus Technology's ability to successfully operate its Global Commercial Platform will affect its ability to increase revenues. Lotus Technology has taken steps to diversify its revenue sources, for example, by entering into the Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK, pursuant to which Lotus Technology is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the U.S., where Lotus Technology International Limited will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute Lotus UK vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Technology.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our ability to maintain strategic partnership with Geely Holding 
summary: Our strategic partnership with Geely Holding is a pivotal element of our business model, providing us with a competitive advantage through rapid commercialization and an asset-light operating framework. This partnership minimizes our need for substantial upfront capital expenditures compared to traditional Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs), allowing us to allocate resources more efficiently.

We have formalized our collaboration through various agreements, which include technology licensing, manufacturing cooperation, and supply frameworks. These agreements not only bolster our operational capabilities but also enhance our ability to manage supply chain risks effectively. By leveraging Geely's established infrastructure and expertise, we can accelerate product development timelines, ensuring that we remain agile in a rapidly evolving market.

This partnership is not just a strategic alliance but a comprehensive integration of resources and knowledge, which positions us favorably against competitors. As we continue to navigate the complexities of the automotive industry, our relationship with Geely will be instrumental in driving innovation and maintaining our market presence. 

Overall, our collaboration with Geely Holding is a cornerstone of our strategy, enabling us to pursue growth opportunities while maintaining financial prudence and operational efficiency.

title: Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control 
summary: The "Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control" chapter highlights the critical role of manufacturers and suppliers in the vehicle development process, emphasizing the necessity of strict adherence to quality standards. A strategic partnership with Geely Holding, the major shareholder, underpins an asset-light business model that accelerates market entry by leveraging Geely's extensive manufacturing capacity, procurement power, capital investment, and operational support.

In 2022, a significant milestone was reached with the establishment of a 10-year manufacturing agreement with Geely Holding for vehicle production. This agreement delineates responsibilities, with the company retaining control over design, development, supplier designation, product announcements, and the maintenance of Lotus brand standards. Conversely, Geely Holding is responsible for sourcing raw materials, production planning, quality control, logistics, and plant operations, ensuring that quality assurance aligns with the company's established framework.

The manufacturing facility located in Wuhan, China, owned by Geely Holding, is specifically tailored for electric vehicles (EVs) and spans over one million square meters. This state-of-the-art plant is equipped with advanced manufacturing technologies, including stamping, welding, painting, assembly, and a dedicated quality inspection center. Additionally, the facility integrates intelligent driving technology for vehicle transport within workshops and features a 3 km track for quality testing, enabling high-speed evaluations of up to 230 km/h and providing various driving experiences for customers.

Overall, this chapter underscores the strategic collaboration with Geely Holding as a cornerstone of the manufacturing and supply chain strategy, ensuring that the company can efficiently produce high-quality vehicles while maintaining brand integrity and meeting market demands.

Similar to other global major automobile manufacturers, Lotus Technology follows its internal process to select suppliers, taking into account quality, cost, and timing. Lotus Technology has a part quality management team that is responsible for managing and ensuring that supplies meet quality standards. Lotus Technology's method for selecting suppliers depends on the nature of the supplies needed. For general parts that are widely available, Lotus Technology examines proposals from multiple suppliers and chooses based on quality and price competitiveness, among other factors. For parts requiring special designs, Lotus Technology reviews design proposals and chooses largely based on design-related factors.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Lotus Overview**

Lotus, established in the U.K. in 1948, is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility, specializing in the design and development of luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic brand. With a rich racing heritage, Lotus has secured 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles, enhancing its reputation in the automotive industry. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, facilitating its transition from a traditional sports car manufacturer to a leader in high-performance battery electric vehicles (BEVs). Geely's support in manufacturing, research and development (R&D), and supply chain management has been instrumental in this transformation.

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is expected to experience significant growth but remains underserved, with only about 10 luxury BEV models currently available compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models. As an early mover in this space, Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, delivering exceptional performance with three available versions, including the Eletre R, which boasts 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Following this, the second model, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024.

Leveraging its storied sports car design heritage, Lotus possesses robust R&D capabilities that provide a competitive advantage, enabling rapid model rollout and scalable production. All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments, and it also ensures access to high-quality components through established supplier relationships.

To enhance customer experience, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 207 stores globally as of June 30, 2024, with plans for further expansion. The company’s global distribution network is supported by a Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK, allowing for efficient marketing and distribution of Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In terms of sales performance, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023 and 4,873 vehicles in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models.

In summary, Lotus is strategically positioned in the growing luxury BEV market, backed by Geely's resources and expertise, with a strong focus on innovation, customer experience, and operational efficiency.

Pursuant to the manufacturing agreement, Lotus Technology commissioned Geely Holding for the production of the Eletre and future models and authorized Geely Holding to access Lotus Technology's technologies for the production of such models. Geely Holding is mainly responsible for the ordering and inspection of raw materials, production planning, production quality control, logistics and transportation of manufactured vehicles, and construction and operation of the manufacturing plant. Particularly, quality control is carried out in accordance with Lotus Technology's quality assurance framework and approved by Geely Holding. In addition, Geely Holding is responsible for obtaining certificates for the manufactured vehicles.

title: Our Collaboration with Geely Holding 
summary: We have entered into a strategic partnership with Geely Holding to enhance our asset-light business model through their extensive ecosystem.
